how i think pontos story would be improved

What is the goal of the story? Its to provide an insight into his character, to make readers connect with him more, to be "another desecrate"

It should have opened with being trapped in ebwynnos, with jago being eternally young, yet having flashbacks of their real history, and a sense that something is deeply wrong (setting up what "villain pontos" looks like, and the conflict)

We don't know the significance of the grasshopper, only that it's jago's only link to his real identity

The story then reveals jago's and pontos's backstory piece by piece, so you come to see how events led to eternal ebwynnos. Extra focus is placed on pontos's character traits - being a control freak, unwilling to accept loss (something which is not done enough in the current version of the story). This foundation is laid so that we can see his breakdown in the second act follows from his tragic character flaws.

Now we know where the grasshopper comes from, jago is finally able to break the illusion and try to escape. The confrontation with pontos plays out as usual. Jago thinks he escapes but actually doesn't

For the last part we switch to the perspective of pontos pulling the strings. Like miryam he is self delusional. Internally he has doubts about the path he has chosen. But the show must go on, ebwynnos must be eternal, or he will be devoured by the demons he unleashed himself
